tools.name: ImageDescription
tools.description: A useful tool that returns a brief description of the input image.

tools.name: OCR
tools.description: This tool can recognize all text on the input image.

outputs.description: OCR results, include bbox in x1, y1, x2, y2 format and the recognized text.

tools.name: RegionAttributeDescription
tools.description: Describe the attribute of a region of the input image.

inputs.name: bbox
inputs.description: The bbox coordinate in the format of `(x1, y1, x2, y2)`

dialogs.content: What type of mutation is shown in the image?

dialogs.thought: First identify the figure type and whether it shows a sequence change across different tissue samples.

content.content: Three side-by-side Sanger sequencing chromatogram panels with base calls at the top and arrows pointing to a nucleotide position. Panels are labeled at the bottom: "SCC", "Dermis", and "Epidermis". The SCC panel shows a mixed peak at the arrowed base compared with the other panels.

dialogs.thought: Read the base calls and labels to determine which nucleotide differs in the SCC sample compared with dermis and epidermis.

arguments.attribute: sequencing peak pattern at the arrowed nucleotide and whether it indicates a point substitution

dialogs.thought: Confirm that the SCC chromatogram has an altered peak at the marked site, consistent with a single-base substitution relative to the normal tissues.

content.content: In the SCC panel at the arrowed position there are overlapping peaks (mixed signal) rather than a single clean peak, consistent with a heteroplasmic or mixed-base call, while the dermis and epidermis panels show a clean 'C' at that position.

dialogs.content: The image shows a C to T point mutation.

whitelist: The image shows a C to T point mutation.
